# fonts

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `fonts`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `fonts.fontconfig.antialiasing` | `null or boolean` | Whether to enable font antialiasing. |
| `fonts.fontconfig.configFile` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | Config files that will be installed to {file}`~/.config/fontconfig/conf.d/`. |
| `fonts.fontconfig.configFile.<name>.enable` | `boolean` | Whether this file should be generated. This option allows specific files to be disabled. |
| `fonts.fontconfig.configFile.<name>.label` | `string` | Label to use for the name of the config file. |
| `fonts.fontconfig.configFile.<name>.priority` | `integer between 0 and 99 (both inclusive)` | Determines the order in which configuration files are loaded. Must be a value within the range of 0-99, where priority 0 is the highest priority and 99 is the lowest. |
| `fonts.fontconfig.configFile.<name>.settings` | `XML value` | Structured attrs of settings in the format of `pkgs.format.xml {}`. The settings are implicitly wrapped inside the `<fontconfig>` tag, i.e. as `{ fontconfig = <settings>; }`. Note that {option}`source` will be derived from {option}`settings` and {option}`text` if they are set; with override priorities being preserved. As a consequence, exactly one of {option}`settings`, {option}`text`, and {option}`source` must be set with highest priority. |
| `fonts.fontconfig.configFile.<name>.source` | `absolute path or (null or absolute path) convertible to it` | Path to the source file. Note that {option}`source` will be derived from {option}`settings` and {option}`text` if they are set; with override priorities being preserved. As a consequence, exactly one of {option}`settings`, {option}`text`, and {option}`source` must be set with highest priority. |
| `fonts.fontconfig.configFile.<name>.target` | `non-empty string` | Path to the target file relative to {file}`${config.xdg.configHome}/fontconfig/conf.d/`. |
| `fonts.fontconfig.configFile.<name>.text` | `null or strings concatenated with "\n"` | Verbatim contents of the config file. Note that {option}`source` will be derived from {option}`settings` and {option}`text` if they are set; with override priorities being preserved. As a consequence, exactly one of {option}`settings`, {option}`text`, and {option}`source` must be set with highest priority. |
| `fonts.fontconfig.defaultFonts.emoji` | `list of string` | Per-user default emoji font(s). Multiple fonts may be listed in case a font does not support all emoji. Note that fontconfig matches color emoji fonts preferentially, so if you want to use a black and white font while having a color font installed (eg. Noto Color Emoji installed alongside Noto Emoji), fontconfig will still choose the color font even when it is later in the list. |
| `fonts.fontconfig.defaultFonts.monospace` | `list of string` | Per-user default monospace font(s). Multiple fonts may be listed in case multiple languages must be supported. |
| `fonts.fontconfig.defaultFonts.sansSerif` | `list of string` | Per-user default sans serif font(s). Multiple fonts may be listed in case multiple languages must be supported. |
| `fonts.fontconfig.defaultFonts.serif` | `list of string` | Per-user default serif font(s). Multiple fonts may be listed in case multiple languages must be supported. |
| `fonts.fontconfig.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable fontconfig configuration. This will, for example, allow fontconfig to discover fonts and configurations installed through {var}`home.packages` and {command}`nix-env`. If Home Manager is installed as a NixOS submodule and {var}`home-manager.useUserPackages` is enabled, this option defaults to the value of NixOS' {var}`fonts.fontconfig.enable`. |
| `fonts.fontconfig.hinting` | `null or one of "none", "slight", "medium", "full"` | The font hinting mode. |
| `fonts.fontconfig.subpixelRendering` | `null or one of "none", "rgb", "bgr", "vertical-rgb", "vertical-bgr"` | The sub-pixel rendering mode. |
